reforms.description: The Israeli government launched the 'Economic Development Plan for the Arab Sector', allocating NIS 15 billion over five years to address barriers to economic development, including education improvements in Arab communities. This plan includes the introduction of a personal account scheme to allow individuals to save time for training purposes and aims to increase the number of college and university students with technology-oriented majors by 40% within five years to better align education with labor market demands.
reforms.package_name: Economic Development Plan for the Arab Sector
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiatives aim to enhance skills development and align education with labor market needs, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Economic Development Plan has been launched and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The allocation of NIS 15 billion and the comprehensive nature of the plan represent a significant structural change in addressing economic disparities.
reforms.source_quote: An important accompaniment to these measures targeted at individuals would be a personal account scheme allowing individuals to save up time for training purposes.

reforms.description: Israel should expand training voucher schemes to include low-skilled workers and disadvantaged groups in low-paying jobs, enhancing their access to skills development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding access to training for low-skilled workers can improve their employability and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal for expanding training vouchers has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed expansion of training voucher schemes represents a significant but narrower reform aimed at improving skills access.
reforms.source_quote: Israeli training voucher schemes should be expanded and their eligibility widened towards low-skilled workers and disadvantaged groups in low-paying jobs.

reforms.description: The government aims to develop light rail systems in the country's three main cities between 2019 and 2023, addressing public transport deficiencies and promoting sustainable urban mobility.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving public transport infrastructure can enhance urban mobility, reduce congestion, and support economic activities, contributing to overall growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The light rail development plan has been announced and is set to be implemented between 2019 and 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: The development of light rail systems represents a significant investment in urban infrastructure, though it is not as transformative as a major overhaul of existing systems.
reforms.source_quote: There is also a plan to develop light rail systems in the country’s three main cities between 2019 and 2023.

reforms.description: The government is expanding the number of hours dedicated to Hebrew teaching in Arabic schools, increasing weekly Hebrew lessons for Arab students and introducing Hebrew language instruction in kindergartens.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing language skills can improve educational outcomes and employability for Arab students, contributing to economic integration and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan to expand Hebrew teaching has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of Hebrew language instruction represents a significant effort to improve educational access and integration for Arab students.
reforms.source_quote: The government is expanding the number of hours dedicated to Hebrew teaching in Arabic schools.

reforms.description: The Water Authority has initiated efforts to stimulate water-saving innovations, particularly by municipal water companies, to combat leaks in the piping system and promote efficient water use.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Stimulating innovations in water management can lead to more efficient resource use and sustainability, positively impacting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Water Authority's efforts to stimulate water-saving innovations are currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to address water management challenges through innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The regulator is pursuing efforts to stimulate water-saving innovations, especially by municipal water companies.
